Lucas Dewey
Experience Contact
Back to experience

2018-2018

Zyris

Product Design Engineer

Resolved manufacturing, assembly, and field-use issues on the Isolite Pro dental medical device, an electromechanical illuminated isolation system with integrated LEDs and connectivity. Work focused on improving molded part fit, sealing reliability, cleanability, assembly quality, and production test support during early customer deployment.

Location Santa Barbara, CA

Highlights

  • Supported an actively shipping dental medical device by diagnosing customer and manufacturing issues across molded plastic, sealing, assembly, and electronics interfaces.
  • Owned design iterations, tolerance analysis, prototype testing, fixture design, and manufacturer coordination for injection molded and electromechanical components.
  • Improved usability and reliability by refining light pipe fit, resolving an air leak failure mode, and updating PCB programming fixtures for production support.

Tools

SolidWorksStatistical tolerance analysisInjection moldingSteel-safe tooling changes3D printingASA prototypingProduction fixture designPCB programming fixturesTest procedure authoringFDA quality system supportManufacturer coordinationElectromechanical troubleshooting

Projects

Work at Zyris

Project

Light pipe fit and usability improvement

Improved the removable light pipe interface after early feedback showed the fit was too tight, causing user frustration and part breakage during cleaning.

Contribution

  • Adjusted dovetail clearances to make the light pipe easier to remove and reinstall
  • Balanced smoother operation with the need to maintain a secure, low-play latched fit
  • Worked within steel-safe tooling constraints to reduce manufacturing risk

Result

Improved daily cleanability and usability while reducing the likelihood of breakage.

Project

Junction box air leak redesign

Diagnosed and corrected an air leak caused by seal material interaction, localized plastic deformation, and air ingress past the dial interface.

Contribution

  • Investigated the interaction between silicone grease, silicone sealing material, and molded plastic deformation
  • Redesigned the seal using an alternate material better suited to the application
  • Added a center cutout to reduce force on the plastic wall while preserving sealing function
  • Used prototype testing to confirm that the seal change also reduced dial removal force, avoiding an unnecessary tooling change

Result

Improved sealing reliability while reducing tooling risk and simplifying the corrective action path.

Project

PCB programming fixture

Designed and updated a semi-automated production fixture for programming PCB assemblies after board hardware changes shifted the programming terminal locations.

Contribution

  • Designed a 3D printed ASA fixture for repeatable PCB programming in a production environment
  • Integrated light pipes to provide visible programming status feedback
  • Updated the fixture interface to accommodate revised button hardware and terminal positions

Result

Maintained production programming throughput and reliability after PCB design changes.
